2606.12342.title: ALIGNBEAM : Inference-Time Alignment Transfer via Cross-Vocabulary Logit Mixing
2606.12342.paper_summary: Domain fine-tuning degrades the safety of large language models: fine-tuned specialists readily comply with harmful prompts framed in domain language. Existing inference-time defenses that mix logits from a safe anchor model require both models to share a vocabulary, which rules them out for the cross-family specialists where safety is most degraded. We present ALIGNBEAM, a training-free method that lifts this restriction by translating anchor logits into the target model's vocabulary token-by-token at each decoding step; a small LLM judge then selects the safest among K candidate continuations. No weights are changed, and the safety-utility trade-off can be tuned at deployment without retraining. Across both cross-vocabulary and same-vocabulary evaluation pairs, ALIGNBEAM substantially raises refusal on adversarial benchmarks while keeping task accuracy and inference overhead within practical bounds. The results show that safety alignment can be transferred between model families at inference time, without touching either model's weights.
2606.12342.paper_summary_zh: 領域微調會降低大型語言模型的安全性：微調的專家容易遵從以領域語言框架呈現的有害提示。現有的推理時防禦措施需要將安全錨點模型的 logits 混合，這要求兩個模型共享詞彙，這使得它們在安全性最差的跨家族專家中無法使用。我們提出了 ALIGNBEAM，一種無需訓練的方法，通過在每個解碼步驟中逐字將錨點 logits 轉換為目標模型的詞彙來解除這一限制；然後，一個小型 LLM 評判者從 K 個候選續集當中選擇最安全的選項。沒有權重被改變，安全性與效用的權衡可以在部署時調整，而無需重新訓練。在跨詞彙和同詞彙的評估對中，ALIGNBEAM 顯著提高了對抗基準的拒絕率，同時保持任務準確性和推理開銷在實際範圍內。結果顯示，安全對齊可以在推理時在模型家族之間轉移，而無需觸及任何模型的權重。
2606.12342.author: Chirag Chawla et.al.
2606.12342.authors: Chirag Chawla, Pratinav Seth, Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
